prevalence.source: Geriatrics epidemiology syntheses (falls) + Brazilian aging cohort context

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; fracture and head injury mortality risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment of fall history, gait/balance (e.g., TUG), orthostatic vitals, vision, medication review, and home risk factors

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; falls risk screening not routine
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: millions of older adults at risk

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often recognized after injury; comprehensive geriatric assessment limited outside large centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multifactorial fall prevention: strength/balance exercise, medication deprescribing, vision correction, home safety, vitamin D when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to physiotherapy and home safety programs and uneven geriatric coverage
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care can implement medication review and basic fall risk screening
